_comment: Starter lane taxonomy for the Gap-5 write-time classifier. Exemplars are CONCRETE, fact-like activity phrasings (not abstract category labels). The classifier scores each lane by the MEAN of a fact's top-K nearest exemplar cosines (multi-prototype, classifyByPrototypes, K=3 default) — NOT a single mean-pooled centroid. So MORE distinct concrete exemplars per lane, covering each lane's sub-topics (e.g. personal: health/family/finance/home/pets/travel; research: papers/experiments/stats/surveys), sharpen routing: a fact matches the relevant exemplar instead of a washed-out average. The 2026-06-07 eval showed this multi-prototype + ~12-exemplar set scores precision 0.977 / recall 0.875 on a representative fixture, beating the prior single-centroid + 6-exemplar set (0.960 / 0.500). Operators SHOULD curate these to their own contexts. 'general' is intentionally absent (spec §3.2): a catch-all of low-confidence facts would re-introduce the D3 false positives the absence-gate prevents.

exemplars: Closed out a sprint ticket and updated the board.
Reviewed a pull request before the release.
Wrote up an incident postmortem for the team.
Planned the quarter roadmap with stakeholders.
Deployed a service and monitored the rollout.
Discussed priorities in the weekly team sync.
Handed off the on-call pager to the next engineer.
Interviewed a candidate for the backend role.
Specced out the new API in a design review.
Investigated a production alert and found the root cause.
Estimated the effort for next sprint's stories.
Triaged incoming bugs and assigned owners.
Made a doctor appointment for a checkup.
Did the weekly grocery shopping and chores.
Went to the gym and tracked the workout.
Paid household bills and reviewed the budget.
Made weekend plans with family and friends.
Took the car in for maintenance.
Booked a weekend getaway and reserved the hotel.
Dropped the kids at daycare in the morning.
Took the cat to the vet for a checkup.
Picked up a prescription refill at the pharmacy.
Fixed the leaky faucet in the bathroom.
Tried a new recipe for dinner.
Read an academic paper on a new method.
Ran an experiment and analyzed the results.
Benchmarked two approaches on a dataset.
Studied the theory behind an algorithm.
Reviewed the methodology of a study.
Investigated a research question in depth.
Summarized findings from a literature survey.
Computed confidence intervals for the results.
Tuned hyperparameters and logged the metrics.
Compared model accuracy across several datasets.
Took notes on prior work in the field.
Designed an experiment to test a hypothesis.
Drafted a new chapter of the story.
Edited prose for clarity and pacing.
Revised the essay structure and argument.
Worked on character voice and dialogue.
Outlined the next section before drafting.
Polished the blog post introduction.
Rewrote a paragraph to tighten the pacing.
Brainstormed titles for the next piece.
Worked on the story's plot and structure.
Proofread the manuscript for typos.
Developed the setting and world details.
Reworked the ending so it lands harder.
